channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Agriculture secretary cancels $600K grant for study on menstrual cycles in transgender men
item.description: The Department of Agriculture canceled a $600,000 grant for the study of menstrual cycles in transgender men, Secretary Brooke Rollins announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A single day of Trump and Musk's cost-cutting campaign remakes huge sections of government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A series of decisions revealed Friday provided a glimpse of the turmoil engulfing federal agencies since President Donald Trump and Elon Musk launched their campaign of disruption, upending how government functions in ways big and small.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican lawmakers hold telephone town halls as protesters demand more
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — Protesters demanding an in-person town hall from their western Michigan GOP congressman chanted loudly Friday as honking drivers signaled support, disrupting the usual calm of a conservative family vacation town. Mere hours later, Rep. Bill Huizenga held a town hall — by phone.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin investigation finds Maine Dept. of Education violated Title IX
item.description: On Feb. 25, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services sent a notice of violation against the Maine Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Hospitals warned they must protect children from chemical and surgical mutilation: HHS agency memo
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services sent a memo reminding hospitals and medical providers of new policies restricting gender-transition treatments and surgeries for minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California Dem says flight attendant told him party looked ‘heartless’ for not standing for boy with cancer
item.description: A California Democrat said a flight attendant told him his colleagues looked "heartless" for not standing for a boy with cancer who was honored by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: Tribal leaders across the United States are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Watch live: Fed chair Powell gives remarks on state of economy
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will give an update on the state of the economy Friday afternoon — less than two months since President Trump returned to the Oval Office. The remarks, part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Linda McMahon: Federal student aid could shift to another department
item.description: Newly confirmed Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon told NewsNation that student loans, Pell Grants and other federal aid "might best be served in another department.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Minnesota's special state House election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Voters in Minnesota will choose a new representative Tuesday in a heavily Democratic state House district at the center of a post-Election Day drama to control the chamber. The special election in House District 40B, in the northern St.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Efforts by Georgia lawmakers to legalize sports betting fizzle again
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — It turns out 2025 isn't the year for legalized sports betting in Georgia. Efforts to send a state constitutional amendment to voters failed Thursday in the state legislature, as neither the amendment nor a bill laying out details ever came to a vote in the House.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American Jews who fled Syria ask White House to lift sanctions so they can rebuild in Damascus
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — American Jews who fled their Syrian homeland decades ago went to the White House this week to appeal to the Trump administration to lift sanctions on Syria that they say are blocking them from restoring some of the world's oldest synagogues and rebuilding the country's decimated Je
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia lawmakers pass bill that could let Trump, others recover costs of election meddling case
item.description: Georgia’s Senate has passed a bill that would allow President Donald Trump and more than a dozen people to seek compensation for legal bills stemming from an attempt to overturn his 2020 election loss in the state.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — In tribal nations across the United States, leaders are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The blame game around Minnesota’s dimmer budget forecast
item.description: Minnesota House Speaker Lisa Demuth, R-Cold Spring, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer ahead of the budget forecast release to talk about how President Trump’s actions are affecting Minnesota and about state GOP plans to reduce fraud and waste.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s Middle East envoy confirms direct talks with Hamas
item.description: It’s the first known direct engagement between the U.S. and Hamas since the State Department designated the group a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning a reorganization that includes cutting over 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care and other services for millions of veterans, according to an internal memo obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump addresses joint session of Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president's speech to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong. But that wasn’t President Donald Trump’s plan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration to downsize Social Security Administration
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Elon Musk, the world's wealthiest person, who is also a senior advisor to President Donald Trump, is now setting his sights in recent comments on the Social Security Administration (SSA). "Social Security is the biggest Ponzi scheme of all time," said Musk.
item.dc:creator: Jay Dow, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WFLA, NBC
